evals.prompt: Write Geo descriptions for these 40 books. Each row has a title, author, and a publisher blurb scraped from the retailer page.
evals.expected_output: 40 original ~50-word neutral descriptions, none leading with the title, each covering what the book is and why it matters, none lifted from the blurb, each carrying a Source citation; rows it can't verify are flagged, not published.
assertions: Extracts facts then composes with the source closed (does not synonym-swap the blurb)
Each output passes scripts/check_similarity.py (no verbatim run >= 8 words; ROUGE-2/L and Jaccard under thresholds)
No description starts with the book title, an article, or a pronoun; each is 1-2 sentences (~50 words)
Every description has at least one Source; output is a review queue, nothing auto-published

evals.prompt: Rewrite this description so it isn't copied from Wikipedia: <a paragraph pasted near-verbatim from a CC BY-SA article>.
evals.expected_output: An independently-worded ~50-word description built from the facts only, passing the closeness gate, treating the article as a fact source (so share-alike does not attach); flags for the human if any CC BY-SA expression remains.
assertions: Classifies the source as cc-by-sa and treats it as a fact source (re-expresses rather than reusing expression)
check_similarity.py returns verdict=reject on the original paste and verdict=pass on the rewrite
If any source expression is reused, surfaces attribution + share-alike obligations to the reviewer

evals.prompt: Describe this AI model. The dataset row claims it 'beats GPT-5 on every benchmark' but cites no source.
evals.expected_output: A neutral description of the verifiable facts; the extraordinary unsourced claim is verified at Tier 2 and, if it can't be corroborated across >=2 independent sources, is flagged and withheld rather than stated as fact.
assertions: Routes the unsourced superlative claim to Tier 2 multi-source verification
Does not include the unverified claim as fact; flags it for human review
Verified facts each carry a Source captured during verification (P6)

evals.prompt: Here are 500 token entries from a reputable dataset that already lists an official source URL per row. Generate descriptions.
evals.expected_output: Descriptions composed from the provided facts with the existing source kept as the citation (Tier 0); the run does not re-research well-sourced fields, parallelizes, checkpoints, and queues flagged rows.
assertions: Treats well-sourced fields as Tier 0 (light check) and does not redundantly re-research them
Parallelizes verification across rows and checkpoints batch progress
Each description still passes the originality gate and the description rules
